- Choose exactly one final label.

- Do not output any text outside the XML tags.
- Follow the condition-specific XML blocks first, then output exactly one final <response> block.
- The final line of the entire answer must be <response>final_class</response>.
- Never omit the final <response> block, even when another explanation block is required.

<features>
- observable feature: flower has deep purple petals
- observable feature: flower has a central cluster of stamens
- observable feature: background contains green leaves
</features>
<kb>
- IF [flower has deep purple petals AND central cluster of stamens] THEN [81]
- IF [flower has yellow petals AND central structure resembling beard] THEN [14]
</kb>
<rule_check>
- Rule 1 fires because the Query Image shows a flower with deep purple petals and a central cluster of stamens, matching the features described in the IF condition for class 81.
</rule_check>
<response>81</response>

<features>
- observable feature: flower has three large yellow petals with distinct markings
- observable feature: flower has three smaller, upright petals
- observable feature: background consists of green, elongated leaves
</features>
<kb>
- IF [flower has three large yellow petals with distinct markings AND three smaller upright petals AND background of green elongated leaves] THEN [14]
- IF [flower has pink and white petals with a dark center AND a visible bud nearby AND background of green grass] THEN [81]
</kb>

- Rule for class 14 fires because the Query Image shows a yellow flower with three large petals, three smaller upright petals, and a background of green elongated leaves, matching the description for class 14.
</rule_check>
<response>14</response>
